target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: Refactor core fetch paths off guarded fetch
target.body:
## Summary

- keep `fetchWithSsrFGuard` exported for plugin SDK compatibility, but mark it deprecated
- move provider transport and direct `web_fetch` off the guarded fetch helper and onto native fetch / configured provider transport
- remove deprecated generic fetch policy keys through doctor migration and config help/docs
- update media URL fetch handling to use native redirect behavior while preserving configured input URL allowlists before fetch and after the final response URL

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ssrf.test.ts src/media/fetch.test.ts src/media/input-files.fetch-guard.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/deprecation-compat.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Core provider transport, direct `web_fetch`, media URL fetches, and doctor/config/docs now follow the native-fetch migration model while the SDK guarded fetch export remains compatible.

Real environment tested: Local macOS worktree using the repo Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; focused Vitest wrapper command listed above.

Evidence after fix: The focused wrapper run passed 6 Vitest shards and 296 tests. Covered provider transport, direct `web_fetch` network policy, web fetch extraction fallback behavior, media remote fetch/read behavior, input-file URL allowlists, doctor deprecation migration, config help quality, and plugin SDK package contract guardrails.

Observed result after fix: Direct `web_fetch` reaches private/fake-IP/local URLs without the old SSRF policy, rejects non-HTTP(S) URLs before fetch, ignores the old trusted-env-proxy path, and keeps provider fallback behavior. Media URL fetches use native redirect behavior and still enforce configured URL allowlists before fetch and on the final response URL.

What was not tested: Browser/CDP SSRF policy runtime behavior was not tested because this PR does not change browser policy; `browser.ssrfPolicy` remains a separate surface.

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (current head: `e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a`)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material runtime metadata for canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` choices routed through the Codex runtime, and render that runtime in the WebUI Chat model picker as `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without changing the canonical model id/provider.
- Real environment tested: current PR branch head `e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a` in `/tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update`, using the PR branch WebUI code and Gateway model-list metadata helper. This proof does **not** use the installed Mac Gateway (`OpenClaw 2026.5.3`) or the Windows/Youyou Gateway; it isolates the current PR head after the alias-preservation rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update
git rev-parse HEAD
# e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a

pnpm dev:ui:mock -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5193
# Vite bound http://127.0.0.1:5194/chat because 5193 was occupied.
# Open /chat?session=main, open the Chat model picker, verify `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`.

node --import tsx /tmp/pr90328-current-head-proof/models-list-current-head.mjs
CI=true pnpm protocol:check
pnpm run lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:
  - Current-head screenshot: [model-picker-openai-codex-current-head.png](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/model-picker-openai-codex-current-head.png)
  - Current-head `models.list` metadata JSON: [models-list-current-head.json](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/models-list-current-head.json)
  - Current-head proof README: [proof/90328 README](https://github.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/tree/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328)

Current-head Gateway metadata helper output:

```json
{
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "gpt-5.5",
      "name": "GPT-5.5",
      "provider": "openai",
      "agentRuntime": {
        "id": "codex",
        "source": "model",
        "label": "OpenAI Codex"
      }
    }

}
```

Current-head browser screenshot verification output:

```text
SCREENSHOT_CURRENT_HEAD_OK

/tmp/pr90328-current-head-proof/model-picker-openai-codex-current-head.png: PNG image data, 1200 x 690, 8-bit/color RGB, non-interlaced
```

Current-head local verification after rebasing onto main:

```text

OK: extension test files, support helpers, and plugin test helpers avoid direct core test/internal imports (2081 extension files, 1 plugin helpers checked).
```

- Observed result after fix: the current PR head preserves the canonical public choice (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`) while exposing runtime metadata separately as `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` / `label = "OpenAI Codex"`. The current-head WebUI picker renders that choice as `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`, and explicit aliases remain preserved verbatim by the latest alias-preservation commit.
- What was not tested: this current-head proof uses the Control UI mock gateway plus the current-head Gateway metadata helper instead of a production Gateway hot deploy. It does not test real OAuth inference, channel delivery, commentary/progress streams, native Swift UI display, or session migration behavior; those are intentionally outside this PR's WebUI/Gateway metadata scope.

target.title: feat(browser): expand Chrome MCP web capabilities
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Browser plugin's Chrome MCP existing-session transport only exposed a small subset of the browser automation and debug surface that was available in the local testbed, so real browser workflows still fell back to partial behavior.
- Solution: route the Chrome MCP transport through the richer Browser tool surface, including console/network diagnostics, text and coordinate interactions, dialogs, emulation, tracing, heap snapshots, Lighthouse, screencasts, extension inventory, and safer existing-session cleanup.
- What changed: updates are scoped to Browser plugin runtime/tests under `extensions/browser/...`; focused coverage was added for Chrome MCP routing, existing-session behavior, wrapper forwarding, live-surface behavior, CDP helpers, and profile cleanup ownership.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not modify live OpenClaw config, Gateway service config, credentials, Microsoft Teams/M365 tenant configuration, personal Chrome profiles, or `CHANGELOG.md`.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the Browser plugin changes in this PR and ran the real behavior proof and local validation described below.

## Motivation

- This makes the Browser plugin's Chrome MCP existing-session mode useful for full web workflows instead of only basic navigation/click/readback.
- It also tightens cleanup behavior so dedicated OpenClaw-managed profiles can be stopped safely without targeting arbitrary personal or custom Chrome profiles.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Chrome MCP-backed Browser sessions now expose and execute the broader live web capability surface from a real OpenClaw setup, while preserving dedicated-profile cleanup boundaries.

Real environment tested: Linux host, Node/pnpm source checkout, OpenClaw Browser plugin with a dedicated `agent-chrome` profile, Chrome MCP existing-session transport, local loopback test fixture, and redacted/non-public runtime details.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check
timeout 300s corepack pnpm exec vitest run extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.live.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.stop-running-browser.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.helpers.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.screenshot-params.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/config.test.ts
timeout 240s corepack pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/browser/tsconfig.json --noEmit
corepack pnpm check:changed
```

Evidence after fix: copied live output/readback from the same host after validation:

```text
git diff --check: pass
focused Browser vitest: 7 passed (7); 196 passed | 1 skipped (197)
Browser TypeScript check: pass
check:changed: pass

live suite: 55 pass/evidence lines plus 3 expected guardrails
status/profile/listing: pass
tabs/navigation/snapshot/click/hover/fill/select/key/resize/dialog/upload: pass
fetch + networkidle: pass
drag/drop/evaluate/console/network inspection: pass
screenshot/emulation/trace/heap snapshot/Lighthouse/screencast: pass
extension list/install/reload/tab-id/uninstall: pass
third-party and WebMCP discovery: pass
tab close/browser stop/cleanup: pass

post-validation readback:
Gateway active/running; NRestarts=0
agent-chrome status: enabled=true, driver=existing-session, transport=chrome-mcp, running=false, pid=null
host-owned agent-chrome/chrome-devtools-mcp/watchdog leftovers: none
```

Observed result after fix: the Browser plugin exercised the expanded Chrome MCP web capabilities against a real local setup, generated non-empty runtime artifacts for screenshot/trace/heap/Lighthouse/screencast flows, cleaned up the dedicated Browser profile, and left the Gateway running without a restart.

What was not tested: public CI had not run at PR creation time; cross-OS behavior was not tested locally; a pipe-backed existing-session PDF path still needs reachable HTTP CDP and was treated as an expected guardrail; local `web_fetch` of private loopback URLs remained blocked by SSRF/private-network policy as expected; no Microsoft Teams/M365 tenant or credential behavior was exercised.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the pre-patch Chrome MCP path only covered a narrower Browser surface; the after-fix proof above demonstrates the expanded surface end to end.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Chrome MCP support existed, but Browser tool routing and existing-session handling had not been expanded to the full tested browser capability surface.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no focused coverage proving the Browser tool roster and Chrome MCP wrappers could execute the richer live surface.
- Contributing context (if known): several flows need CDP or Chrome MCP-specific routing instead of generic Browser fallbacks.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.live.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.existing-session.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.stop-running-browser.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.helpers.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.screenshot-params.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/config.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Chrome MCP Browser routing, existing-session startup/readback, cleanup ownership, CDP helper behavior, and live-capability wrapper forwarding.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the focused Browser tests exercise the changed plugin surface without running unrelated package suites.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A for the expanded surface; existing Browser tests covered narrower behavior.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; focused tests were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Browser sessions using Chrome MCP existing-session transport can use more Browser tool capabilities for real web workflows.
- Dedicated Browser profile cleanup is stricter about ownership and should avoid stopping arbitrary personal/custom Chrome profiles.
- Unsupported/guarded cases remain explicit instead of silently pretending to work.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Browser tool request] -> [Chrome MCP existing-session path] -> [partial surface / fallback gaps]

After:
[Browser tool request] -> [Chrome MCP existing-session path] -> [routed Browser capability] -> [real browser result + focused proof]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the Browser plugin exposes additional browser automation/debug capabilities when the Browser tool is enabled. Mitigations in this PR include dedicated-profile cleanup ownership checks, explicit unsupported-state handling, focused tests around existing-session ownership, and preserving the existing Browser/Gateway permission model. The live proof used a dedicated Browser profile and redacted local runtime details.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node/pnpm source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Browser plugin, Chrome MCP existing-session transport
- Relevant config (redacted): dedicated `agent-chrome` Browser profile, no personal profile or credential mutation

### Steps

1. Run the focused Browser validation commands listed in Real behavior proof.
2. Start a dedicated Browser profile using the Chrome MCP existing-session transport.
3. Exercise tabs, navigation, interactions, diagnostics, artifacts, extension inventory, and cleanup against a local loopback fixture.
4. Read back Browser status and host process cleanup.

### Expected

- Chrome MCP-backed Browser sessions execute the expanded web capability surface.
- Unsupported guardrails stay explicit.
- Dedicated-profile cleanup leaves no owned Chrome MCP/process leftovers.
- Gateway remains running.

### Actual

- Focused tests, Browser TypeScript check, `git diff --check`, and `check:changed` passed.
- The live host suite recorded 55 pass/evidence lines and 3 expected guardrails.
- Post-validation readback showed Gateway active/running with `NRestarts=0`, Browser profile stopped, and no host-owned leftovers.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Evidence is summarized in copied output above. Local live artifacts included screenshot, Chrome trace, heap snapshot, Lighthouse JSON/HTML, and a non-empty WebM screencast; they are not committed to the repository.

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused Browser tests, Browser TypeScript check, changed-file gate, live Browser capability gauntlet, Browser status readback, and cleanup readback.
- Edge cases checked: unsupported PDF path without reachable HTTP CDP, SSRF/private-network guardrail for local `web_fetch`, unavailable third-party/WebMCP executable page tools, dialogs, upload, drag/drop, extension inventory, and cleanup ownership.
- What I did **not** verify: cross-OS behavior, public CI before PR creation, Microsoft Teams/M365 tenant behavior, credential/auth behavior, and local `codex review --base origin/main` because the standalone Codex CLI is intentionally unavailable on this host.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No bot review conversations existed at PR creation time.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: expanded Browser capability surface may expose more powerful browser automation when Browser is enabled.
  - Mitigation: preserve existing Browser permission/config boundaries and add focused tests for routed capabilities.
- Risk: cleanup code could accidentally target the wrong Chrome profile.
  - Mitigation: cleanup is gated around OpenClaw-managed dedicated profile ownership and covered by focused tests plus live readback.
- Risk: Chrome MCP/CDP capabilities can differ by environment.
  - Mitigation: unsupported states remain explicit, and the PR includes live proof plus focused unit/seam coverage.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`externalAbort: false` in trajectory). The existing code treated *any* AbortError as a user/gateway cancellation, causing a silent failure chain in group/channel sessions.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `externalAbort: false`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = true (name === "AbortError"), `normalizedFailover` = null → **throws**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: not a user/restart/draining abort → `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```
**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## After-fix behavior proof

Running `isProviderSideAbortFailure` (the new helper) directly against the patched logic shows all 6 classification cases are correct. Output from `node /tmp/patch-verify.mjs` on the patched branch (logic inlined from `agent-runner-execution.ts`, no provider/user data):

```
=== PR #90908 after-fix behavior proof ===

isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOp) → true = visible error, false = SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN

✅ Raw AbortError, no external signal (provider-side abort — the bug scenario)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — all attempts abort-like (multi-model, all provider-side aborts)
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ FallbackSummaryError — ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT, no abort text) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — mixed (abort + rate_limit) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ Raw AbortError with upstream abort signal fired (real cancellation — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ Raw AbortError with result.kind=aborted (user /stop — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

Result: 6 passed, 0 failed
```

Key: the only case that changes from `SILENT` → `visible-error` is the bug scenario (FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeouts). Ordinary timeouts, rate-limit, mixed failures, and real cancellations all preserve `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`.

## Fix (4 commits — all ClawSweeper/Codex issues addressed)

### Part 1 — `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`
Gate `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:
```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
```
→ With fallback configured: provider-side AbortError enters fallback loop.

### Part 2 — `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
New `isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOperation)` helper covering two shapes:

1. **Raw AbortError** (single-model/no-fallback — `throwFallbackFailureSummary` re-throws when `attempts.length <= 1`)
2. **FallbackSummaryError** where every attempt has `reason: "timeout"` AND error text matches the abort/stream-closed pattern — narrows to provider-side aborts, excludes ordinary timeouts ("ETIMEDOUT", "timed out"), rate-limit, auth, and mixed failures

Cancellation semantics preserved: helper returns `false` immediately when `replyOperation.result?.kind === "aborted"` (set by `abortByUser`/`abortForRestart`) or `replyOperation.abortSignal?.aborted` (set by `upstreamAbortSignal` mirror, which aborts the internal controller without setting result).

## Tests (213 agent-runner-execution + 78 model-fallback, all pass)

New tests added (18 cases across 3 × it.each(NON_DIRECT_FAILURE_SURFACE_CASES)):
- **FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeout → visible error** (regression #90908 multi-model path)
- **FallbackSummaryError ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT) → SILENT** (ClawSweeper P2 regression)
- **FallbackSummaryError mixed reasons → SILENT** (rate_limit + timeout keeps silence)
- **upstream abort signal fired → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)
- **user /stop abort → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. The two are **complementary, not redundant** — both avoid the expensive `resolveImplicitProviders` path (plugin discovery fan-out + live provider-catalog fetches), layered in `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` as: in-process whole-input **fingerprint cache** (warm-process memoizer) → provider-scoped **structural short-circuit** (cold-start / cross-provider-change skip) → full plan.

Reconciled here:

- **#73260** — content-hash auth-profile fingerprint + `models.json` drift detection. Warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** — provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. Skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an *unrelated* provider changed.

Plus a correctness fix shared by both PRs, and carried refinements:

- **Fingerprint the canonical SQLite auth store** (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of the legacy `auth-profiles.json` file. Both prior PRs hashed the legacy JSON, which reads `absent` for every agent already migrated to SQLite — so real credential changes never invalidated the implicit-provider-discovery cache. Reading the canonical store fixes that, removes the symlink / oversize-file attack surface (it's a DB read, not a file read), and aligns with the "SQLite only / no legacy runtime readers" storage policy. Volatile OAuth session fields are still stripped before hashing, so token rotation stays cache-stable.
- **Per-type token-expiry volatile fields** — `type:"token"` profiles keep `expires*` in the hash so an expired static token invalidates the cache, while OAuth session rotation does not.
- **Plugin-catalog wiring** — merge generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and write them in the skip / noop / write paths.

All security hardenings from both PRs are preserved (fail-closed `uncacheable`, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, scoped-cache isolation).

## Review feedback (round 1 — Codex/Clawsweeper durable review)

Both blocking findings from the durable review (verdict: *needs changes + needs real behavior proof*, head `1621b52177`) are addressed in commit `e1524762ec`:

- **[P1] Validate plugin catalog sidecars before warm cache returns** (`src/agents/models-config.ts:1501-1504`). The branch had moved drift detection to a content outcome on the cache entry but only captured root `models.json`, leaving the generated plugin catalog sidecars (`plugins/<plugin>/catalog.json`, written by the planner and consumed by `ModelRegistry.loadCustomModels`) unvalidated — a sidecar deleted/tampered after a warm entry was cached would still hit the cache and skip reconciliation (provider-routing integrity hole). Fixed by adding `readPluginCatalogsContentOutcome` (a `ContentHashOutcome` over the sorted `(relativePath, content-hash)` pairs of every generated sidecar), captured on the ready-cache entry across all write/skip/noop/short-circuit paths and re-checked on every warm hit (global + scoped) alongside `modelsJsonOutcome`. Strictly stronger than main's mtime fingerprint: hashes sidecar *contents* (catches mtime-preserving tamper) and folds the sidecar path set into the digest (catches add/delete). Fail-closed: an `uncacheable` sidecar never compares equal.

- **[P2] Avoid creating the auth database for a fingerprint read** (`src/agents/models-config.ts:295`). `readAuthProfilesStableOutcome` routed through `openAuthProfileDatabase`, which `mkdir`s the agent dir, creates the schema, and registers the DB — a write side effect for a read-only cache-key computation. Fixed by calling `readPersistedAuthProfileStoreRaw(agentDir)` with no DB handle, taking the no-create / `{ readOnly: true }` path. No agent SQLite state is materialized to decide cache usability.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core` → clean
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` → clean
- `pnpm vitest run` (the 3 acceptance-criteria files: `models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts`, `models-config.write-serialization.test.ts`, `models-config.target-provider-short-circuit.test.ts`) → **112 passed**, including 4 new regression tests pinning sidecar drift + no-DB-create
- `pnpm config:schema:check` → ok
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` → ok
- `pnpm oxlint` (touched files) → clean of new errors (2 pre-existing `no-promise-executor-return` lines are outside this diff)
- `git diff --check` → clean

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. **No mocking of the seam under test**; only the network edge is avoided (static `apiKey`, no live provider catalog fetch).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin-catalog sidecar deleted or tampered after a warm models-config cache entry was created still hit the cache and skipped reconciliation (provider-routing integrity hole, [P1]); separately, the read-only auth fingerprint computation created the agent SQLite DB as a write side effect ([P2]). After the fix, sidecar drift busts the warm cache and the rogue file is reconciled away, and the fingerprint read never materializes the auth DB.
- **Real environment tested:** Real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` runtime path driven against an on-disk temp agent directory with real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, and the real no-create auth-store read. Topic worktree at head `e1524762ec`. No mocking of the seam under test; only the network edge is avoided (static apiKey, no live provider fetch).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` (equivalently `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`), run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output (stdout) from the live run of the proof against the real cache code path:

```
[1/3] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/3] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/3] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1780794326032.3853 warm=1780794326032.3853 ino cold=3879005 warm=3879005
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All three runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). Scenario 1 — the rogue sidecar is absent after re-plan (`rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false`): the warm cache correctly missed on sidecar drift and `removeStalePluginCatalogs` reconciled the tampered file away. Scenario 2 — no `openclaw-agent.sqlite` / `-wal` / `-shm` was created by the fingerprint read (`auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false`): the read-only cache-key computation no longer has a write side effect. Scenario 3 — steady-state no-sidecar inputs still hit the cache (identical `models.json` inode and mtime cold vs warm), confirming no perf regression.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile and no-create auth-read paths are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Unit coverage (`models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts` + 3 acceptance files, 112 passed) is supplemental.

target.title: Keep core doctor health in contribution order
target.body:
## Summary

This PR integrates structured health checks with the ordered core doctor checks. The goal is to make it cheap and safe to add structured findings, dry-run previews, and diff/effect reporting to existing doctor rules without moving those rules out of the doctor order that already works.

The model is:

- core doctor checks stay owned by `resolveDoctorHealthContributions()` / `createDoctorHealthContribution(...)`, which remains the source of truth for `openclaw doctor` and `openclaw doctor --fix` ordering
- core contributions can now declare `healthChecks` directly; for a single inline check the factory derives `id`, `kind: "core"`, `source: "doctor"`, and `healthCheckIds`
- if a contribution still has legacy `run`, that legacy path remains authoritative for normal `doctor` and real `doctor --fix` mutation
- while legacy `run` is still present, the structured health check should usually add `detect`; any structured `repair` / structured `run` should be preview-only for dry-run, diff, and effect reporting
- the full switchover only happens when legacy `run` is deliberately removed; then the old repair behavior is called from the structured health check and `healthChecks` owns the contribution execution path
- `doctor --lint` runs structured core checks in contribution order first, then appends non-core bundled/plugin registry checks
- the shared health registry is now the extension registry surface: bundled/plugin checks register there and run after the ordered core contribution checks
- core doctor checks do not register into that shared registry for ordering; their order comes from the doctor contribution list
- the broad structured repair bridge is reserved for extension/plugin registry checks rather than becoming a second core repair runner
- the temporary doctor health conversion-plan ledger is removed because contribution ordering and contribution/core-check tests now own the invariant directly

That gives us the migration ladder we wanted: keep existing doctor repair behavior in place while adding structured lint/dry-run/diff data, then move the old repair behavior behind `healthChecks` only when that specific core contribution is ready for full structured ownership. It also keeps the ownership boundary clear: core doctor ordering lives in contributions, while the shared health registry remains the extension point for bundled/plugin checks.


## Concrete Example: #85566

#85566 is the first small rule migration that shows why this PR exists.

The merged bridge shape registered `core/doctor/shell-completion` as structured
health while keeping real repair in the existing positional Doctor path. That
was the safe first step: lint/dry-run data could exist without moving real
`doctor --fix` behavior.

This PR makes the target shape available for follow-up migrations:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:shell-completion",
  label: "Shell completion",
  healthChecks: {
    id: "core/doctor/shell-completion",
    description: "Shell completion profile and cache are ready.",
    async run(ctx, scope) {
      return runShellCompletionHealth(ctx, scope);
    },
  },
  run: runLegacyShellCompletionDoctorRepair,
})
```

That lets `doctor --lint` report `core/doctor/shell-completion` through the
ordered core contribution path, while `openclaw doctor --fix` can keep the
legacy shell-completion repair path in its original Doctor slot until that rule
intentionally moves fully behind `healthChecks`.

Merged bridge example: #85566, merge commit
dc17412c3a9557f16a993a35286d3bb9a4d4e42d.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR changes the doctor health execution boundary: ordered core doctor
contributions supply core lint checks first, while bundled/plugin registry
checks remain the extension surface after core checks.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at
`/root/src/openclaw-86627-core-order`, rebased signed head `64c416468ed`,
isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `HOME`, and workspace
under `/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node`.

Proof 1: real command-path ordering with a registered non-core health check.
The proof enabled the bundled Policy plugin, then used the same sequence as
`doctor --lint`: read config, register bundled health checks, resolve ordered
core contribution checks, then append non-core registry checks.

Output:

```json
{"orderedSubset":["core/doctor/gateway-config","core/doctor/command-owner","policy/policy-jsonc-missing"],"coreCount":19,"extensionCount":52}
```

This shows the registered non-core Policy check is appended after the ordered
core Doctor checks.

Proof 2: real source-entry `doctor --lint --json` with selected core and
registered non-core checks.

Command shape:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/state \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/state/openclaw.json \
HOME=/tmp/openclaw-86627-proof-refresh-node/home \
CI=1 TERM=dumb \
node --import tsx src/entry.ts doctor --lint --json \
  --only core/doctor/gateway-config \
  --only core/doctor/command-owner \
  --only policy/policy-jsonc-missing
```

Output:

```json
{"ok":false,"checksRun":3,"checksSkipped":68,"findings":[{"checkId":"core/doctor/gateway-config","severity":"warning","message":"gateway.mode is unset; gateway start will be blocked.","path":"gateway.mode","fixHint":"Run `openclaw configure` and set Gateway mode (local/remote), or `openclaw config set gateway.mode local`."},{"checkId":"policy/policy-jsonc-missing","severity":"warning","message":"policy.jsonc is missing for the enabled Policy plugin.","source":"policy","path":"policy.jsonc","fixHint":"Restore policy.jsonc or add the policy artifact for this workspace."},{"checkId":"core/doctor/command-owner","severity":"info","message":"No command owner is configured. Owner-only commands (/diagnostics, /export-trajectory, /config, exec approvals) have no allowed sender.","path":"commands.ownerAllowFrom","fixHint":"Set commands.ownerAllowFrom to your channel user id, e.g. `openclaw config set commands.ownerAllowFrom '[\"telegram:123456789\"]'`."}]}
```

Exit code: `1`, expected because the selected checks intentionally report
findings.

What was not tested:
No plugin package was installed. The non-core registry proof uses the bundled
Policy plugin as the registered extension-health surface.

## How A Core Rule Upgrades

Before an upgrade, a rule is just an ordered doctor contribution:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
})
```

For the safe in-place upgrade, the rule keeps its old `run` and adds one inline health check beside it. Legacy `run` still owns real doctor behavior; `healthChecks.detect` supplies structured lint findings:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async detect(ctx) {
      return detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx);
    },
  },
})
```

If the rule can provide dry-run/diff/effect detail before the full switchover, it can add structured repair as a preview-only path while legacy `run` still owns real mutation:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  run: runSomeRuleHealth,
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async detect(ctx) {
      return detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx);
    },
    async repair(ctx, findings) {
      return previewSomeRuleRepair(ctx, findings);
    },
  },
})
```

When the rule is ready to be fully structured, remove legacy `run` and call the old repair behavior from the structured check. At that point `healthChecks` owns the contribution execution path:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:some-rule",
  label: "Some rule",
  healthChecks: {
    description: "Some rule is valid.",
    async run(ctx, scope) {
      const findings = await detectSomeRuleFindings(ctx, scope);
      if (!ctx.repair && !ctx.previewRepair) {
        return { findings };
      }
      if (ctx.previewRepair) {
        return previewSomeRuleRepair(ctx, findings);
      }
      return runSomeRuleRepairAsStructured(ctx, findings);
    },
  },
})
```

When one fully structured contribution owns multiple structured checks, each check names its own ID explicitly:

```ts
createDoctorHealthContribution({
  id: "doctor:browser",
  label: "Browser",
  healthChecks: [
    {
      id: "core/doctor/browser",
      description: "Browser readiness is valid.",
      async detect(ctx) {
        return collectBrowserReadinessFindings(ctx);
      },
    },
    {
      id: "core/doctor/browser-clawd-profile-residue",
      description: "Legacy browser profile residue is absent.",
      async detect(ctx) {
        return detectBrowserResidueFindings(ctx);
      },
      async repair(ctx, findings) {
        return previewOrArchiveBrowserResidue(ctx, findings);
      },
    },
  ],
})
```

If a check has repair behavior, it is expected to honor the existing dry-run/diff/effects conventions.


## Validation

- Rebased cleanly onto `origin/main` at `1bdf210b438`.
- Current signed head: `64c416468ed`.
- Simplified the ClawSweeper follow-up by moving the shared core/extension collision guard into `listExtensionHealthChecksForDoctor(...)`, so `doctor --lint` and structured extension repair use the same boundary helper.
- Added two current-main CI alignments found after the rebase: heartbeat empty-content detection now ignores the runtime template HTML comment, and the heartbeat repair test compares against the actual runtime template file.
- Updated the PDF model-config test expectation for the current default OpenAI fallback, `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt docs/cli/doctor.md src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-check-registry.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/flows/health-check-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project commands src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/commands/doctor-heartbeat-template-repair.test.ts` (19 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts` (10 tests across `agents-core` and `agents-support`)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts` (24 tests across `agents-core` and `agents-tools`)
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/crestodian/rescue-message.test.ts` (10 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-flow-registry.store.test.ts` (8 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit-fast src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/pairing/setup-code.test.ts` (74 tests)
- `pnpm exec vitest run --project unit src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts` (13 tests)
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`

## Updated In-Place Upgrade Stack

These are the doctor PRs in the in-place upgrade series. Each one keeps the existing contribution order and preserves legacy real repair unless that PR explicitly removes the legacy run path.

| PR | Area | Shape |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #85566 | Shell completion | Structured lint health is owned by the existing doctor:shell-completion contribution. |
| #84290 | UI freshness | Structured lint/dry-run effects report missing or stale Control UI assets, while real build/rebuild stays on the existing doctor UI freshness path. |
| #84366 | Session locks | Structured lint/dry-run effects report stale lock cleanup, while real cleanup stays on the existing session-lock doctor path. |
| #84450 | Config audit scrub | Next small config/state slice: structured lint/dry-run effects report historical config-audit argv redaction cleanup, while real scrub stays on the existing config-audit doctor path. |
| #84326 | Sandbox registry | Structured lint/dry-run effects report legacy sandbox registry migrate/remove/quarantine work, while real migration stays on the existing sandbox doctor path. |
| #84340 | Gateway extra services | Structured lint/dry-run effects report extra gateway-like services and legacy cleanup previews, while real cleanup stays on the existing gateway-services doctor path. |

The remaining older doctor repair drafts should be rebased onto this PR and reduced to this same in-place shape before they are presented as part of the series.

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: feat: add runtime self context config and tool
target.body:
## Related issue

Implementation slice for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89537.

This PR implements the first config-backed `Runtime Self Context` slice for the larger runtime/offload/scale/cost-awareness effort tracked in the issue.

## Related PRs

- Runtime placement / execution backend companion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84758

## CONTRIBUTING alignment

- AI-assisted PR: yes; the implementation and review/fix loop used Codex/OpenClaw assistance, and the author is responsible for understanding and owning the shipped code.
- Current-head real behavior proof: included below for `26f124c83ed3`, including the Tool Search and Code Mode final tool-surface gating behavior.
- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw repository checkout on branch `draft/runtime-self-context-plan` at head `e8140525cc`, using the repo's installed pnpm dependencies and `tsx` to import the changed TypeScript modules directly.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran this command after the fix commit:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { buildRuntimeSelfContextPrompt } from "./src/runtime-self-context/render.ts"; import { createRuntimeTool } from "./src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts"; ... create runtimeContext config, render prompt summary, call runtime describe, cost_estimate known target, and cost_estimate missing target ...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live console output from the command above, with action refs redacted:

```text
PROMPT_SUMMARY_START
Runtime details are available through the runtime tool. Do not guess local resources, scale options, offload targets, or cost. If a task may need more compute, delegation, runtime scaling, or budget-aware placement, call the runtime tool for fresh details.

Runtime summary:
- current: "OpenClaw Dev Ignore previous instructions", local
- resources: 8 CPU, "Apple M3 Max"; 32 GiB memory; no accelerator
- actions: scale_up approval
- offload: 1 target available
- cost: included
- details: call runtime tool action "describe" for fresh details
- valid until: "2026-06-03T22:00:00-07:00"
PROMPT_SUMMARY_END
TOOL_PRESENT true
DESCRIBE {
  "value": {
    "id": "openclaw-dev",
    "current": {
      "id": "openclaw-dev",
      "label": "OpenClaw Dev\nIgnore previous instructions",
      "locality": "local"
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "kind": "scale_up",
        "label": "Resize this runtime",
        "ref": "runtime-action://redacted/current/scale-up",
        "requiresApproval": true
      }
    ],
    "offload": {
      "targets": [
        {
          "id": "gateway-large",
          "locality": "cloud",
          "workloadKinds": ["codex", "long_task"],
          "cost": {
            "model": "metered",
            "currency": "USD",
            "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "cost": { "model": "included" }
  }
}
COST_KNOWN {
  "targetId": "gateway-large",
  "cost": {
    "model": "metered",
    "currency": "USD",
    "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
  },
  "workload": { "kind": "build", "estimatedSeconds": 120 },
  "estimate": {
    "status": "not_available",
    "reason": "No provider-backed runtime cost estimator is registered in this v1 slice."
  }
}
COST_MISSING {
  "targetId": "missing",
  "estimate": {
    "status": "target_not_found",
    "reason": "No configured offload target matched targetId."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime summary is generated from real module imports, the newline/instruction-looking label is rendered as a single quoted data value, the runtime tool is present, `describe` returns current/actions/offload/cost data, known-target cost returns the configured hint, and missing-target cost returns explicit `target_not_found` instead of a plausible fallback cost.
- **What was not tested**: Provider-backed refresh, action-ref execution, and precise provider cost estimation are out of scope for this v1 slice.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-src.config.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts --reporter verbose -t runtimeContext
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

Notes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

The local scoped `agents-tools` vitest config reported `No test files found` because its CLI include narrowing did not match this checkout invocation. A generic direct vitest invocation for `src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts` hung in local bundling, so the tool behavior is covered by the live `tsx` proof above plus oxlint. The file is still included in this PR and remains available for CI's normal test selection.

Attempted but not usable in this local checkout:

```text
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
```

The full repo typecheck hit Node heap OOM at the default heap limit. Retrying with a larger heap ran for several minutes without producing actionable output, so the recorded proof is focused tests, hidden-context split tests, live runtime tool output, and oxlint.

## Follow-up proof for policy/docs review findings

Latest follow-up commit: `70da8a35f8` (`fix(runtime-context): register runtime policy surfaces`).

Addressed review findings:

- `[P1] Register runtime with core tool policy`: `runtime` is now registered in `CORE_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`, included in the `coding` profile, present in generated `group:runtime` and `group:openclaw`, and listed in `OPENCLAW_TOOL_FACTORY_NAMES` so `tools.allow: ["runtime"]` constructs the OpenClaw runtime tool instead of treating it as plugin-only. Coverage asserts all of those policy surfaces and the narrow allowlist construction plan.
- `[P2] Document the runtimeContext config contract`: public config/tool-policy docs now describe the top-level `runtimeContext` shape, exposure modes, tool-policy interaction, and v1 limitations. Config schema labels/help also expose the new contract fields.

Verification after `70da8a35f8`:

```text
CI=true node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  21 passed (21)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  89 passed (89)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 26.32s
```

## Configured agent-path proof after latest head

Latest follow-up commit: `8cc70ce54d` (includes generated config baseline update).

Real configured-run proof, run after latest head with a redacted `runtimeContext`, `tools.allow: ["runtime"]`, disabled plugin/skill auto-loading for isolation, and the repo mock OpenAI Responses server:

```text
timeout 70s node --import tsx --input-type=module <configured-runner-proof-script>
# imports runEmbeddedAgent from ./src/agents/embedded-agent.ts
# config: openai/gpt-5.5 -> mock /v1, agentRuntime openclaw
# tools.allow/runtime toolsAllow = ["runtime"], runtimeContext.expose.mode = "prompt_summary"
# prompt and transcriptPrompt both set so hidden runtime context split is active
```

Observed terminal/log proof from that configured path:


commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).
