target.title: fix(anthropic): resolve Claude Haiku 4.5 static catalog refs
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90088.

- Adds the missing native Anthropic API-key static catalog entry for Claude Haiku 4.5.
- Maps rolling/dotted Haiku 4.5 refs to Anthropic's dated API id, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`.
- Keeps this scoped to the `anthropic` API-key provider; `claude-cli` parity is intentionally out of scope.
- Preserves manifest `maxTokens` and `mediaInput` metadata through the agent catalog boundary, closing a pre-existing metadata propagation gap exposed by this model row.

Reviewers should focus on static catalog resolution, Anthropic alias normalization, pricing-cache lookup for the dated Haiku id, and cron/session model override canonicalization.

## Linked context

Closes #90088.

Related PRs checked:
- #87719 changes Haiku fallback/migration behavior, but does not add the missing native Anthropic catalog row.
- #88587 improves prefixed Anthropic normalization context, but does not add `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` to the native Anthropic static catalog.

Direct collision check:
- #90106 adds dated catalog rows, but does not resolve the rolling `claude-haiku-4-5` alias and has no focused regression proof.
- #90116 adds rolling and dated manifest rows, but does not cover dotted aliases, canonical pricing, runtime catalog tests, or proof.
- This PR keeps one canonical dated Anthropic row, uses the existing Opus/Sonnet alias-to-canonical-id pattern, and verifies dated, rolling, dotted, provider-qualified, pricing, cron/session, and catalog metadata paths.

## Canonicalization choice

Rolling and dotted user refs (`claude-haiku-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4.5`) canonicalize to Anthropic's dated API id, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, matching the existing Anthropic Opus/Sonnet alias normalization pattern. This avoids duplicate catalog rows for one vendor model and keeps runtime/pricing ids unambiguous.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`, `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`, and the dated API id now resolve to the bundled native Anthropic static catalog row instead of falling through as an unknown model.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `codex/issue-90088-anthropic-haiku-catalog`, after `pnpm build` completed `runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin metadata`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source-runtime resolution probe with `node --import tsx` against `resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`, plus the focused regression suites listed below.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Console output from the after-patch source-runtime probe:

```text
claude-haiku-4-5 -> anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 input=text,image context=200000 max=64000
claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 -> anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 input=text,image context=200000 max=64000
anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 -> anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 input=text,image context=200000 max=64000
```

- Observed result after fix: Each tested Haiku 4.5 ref resolved to `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` with text/image input, 200k context, and 64k max output from the bundled Anthropic manifest metadata.
- What was not tested: Live Anthropic completion was not tested because no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` was present locally. `claude-cli` catalog parity was not changed or tested because #90088 is scoped to the native Anthropic API-key provider.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The real behavior proof is local source-runtime resolution, not a paid Anthropic API request. Pricing behavior is covered by the gateway pricing-cache regression using OpenRouter-style catalog data.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The issue/review identified that the native Anthropic static catalog lacked the Haiku 4.5 dated row, so rolling Haiku 4.5 refs could not resolve to a bundled native Anthropic row.

## Tests and validation

Focused tests run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.cron-model-override.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.cron-model-override.test.ts`

Supplemental gates run:

- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cron/isolated-agent/run.cron-model-override.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed --staged`

Regression coverage added/updated:

- Static catalog resolution for dated, rolling, dotted, and provider-qualified Haiku 4.5 refs.
- Agent model-ref fallback expectations now use the dated canonical id.
- Cron isolated-agent session override expectation now uses the dated canonical id.
- Manifest catalog loading asserts Haiku metadata, including image input, reasoning, 200k context, and 64k max output.
- Gateway pricing cache now proves dated Haiku 4.5 resolves to non-zero pricing via the `version-dots` transform.

Known unrelated local gate failures:

- `pnpm format:check` fails repo-wide on pre-existing unrelated files.
- `pnpm lint:all` fails repo-wide on unrelated generated/app/test files, mostly `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/.../CanvasA2UI/a2ui.bundle.js`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Native Anthropic Haiku 4.5 refs now resolve from the bundled static catalog.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model id canonicalization and pricing lookup for Anthropic Haiku 4.5.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change follows the existing Anthropic opus/sonnet alias pattern, adds focused normalization/static-catalog/model-resolution tests, and adds pricing-cache coverage for the dated Haiku id.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer/reviewer review.

Live Anthropic API proof is not included because no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` was available locally; the included proof exercises the source-runtime static catalog path that caused #90088.

Addressed review items:

- Updated stale `model-ref-shared`, embedded runner model, and cron isolated-agent expectations.
- Expanded static catalog alias coverage.
- Proved non-zero pricing-cache lookup for dated Haiku 4.5.
- Added comments justifying all-provider `maxTokens`/`mediaInput` propagation and core alias duplication.
- Ran the changed-lane gate locally in child mode after default delegated runner sanity checks failed locally.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`externalAbort: false` in trajectory). The existing code treated *any* AbortError as a user/gateway cancellation, causing a silent failure chain in group/channel sessions.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `externalAbort: false`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = true (name === "AbortError"), `normalizedFailover` = null → **throws**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: not a user/restart/draining abort → `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```
**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## After-fix behavior proof

Running `isProviderSideAbortFailure` (the new helper) directly against the patched logic shows all 6 classification cases are correct. Output from `node /tmp/patch-verify.mjs` on the patched branch (logic inlined from `agent-runner-execution.ts`, no provider/user data):

```
=== PR #90908 after-fix behavior proof ===

isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOp) → true = visible error, false = SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN

✅ Raw AbortError, no external signal (provider-side abort — the bug scenario)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — all attempts abort-like (multi-model, all provider-side aborts)
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ FallbackSummaryError — ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT, no abort text) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — mixed (abort + rate_limit) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ Raw AbortError with upstream abort signal fired (real cancellation — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ Raw AbortError with result.kind=aborted (user /stop — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

Result: 6 passed, 0 failed
```

Key: the only case that changes from `SILENT` → `visible-error` is the bug scenario (FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeouts). Ordinary timeouts, rate-limit, mixed failures, and real cancellations all preserve `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`.

## Fix (4 commits — all ClawSweeper/Codex issues addressed)

### Part 1 — `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`
Gate `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:
```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
```
→ With fallback configured: provider-side AbortError enters fallback loop.

### Part 2 — `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
New `isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOperation)` helper covering two shapes:

1. **Raw AbortError** (single-model/no-fallback — `throwFallbackFailureSummary` re-throws when `attempts.length <= 1`)
2. **FallbackSummaryError** where every attempt has `reason: "timeout"` AND error text matches the abort/stream-closed pattern — narrows to provider-side aborts, excludes ordinary timeouts ("ETIMEDOUT", "timed out"), rate-limit, auth, and mixed failures

Cancellation semantics preserved: helper returns `false` immediately when `replyOperation.result?.kind === "aborted"` (set by `abortByUser`/`abortForRestart`) or `replyOperation.abortSignal?.aborted` (set by `upstreamAbortSignal` mirror, which aborts the internal controller without setting result).

## Tests (213 agent-runner-execution + 78 model-fallback, all pass)

New tests added (18 cases across 3 × it.each(NON_DIRECT_FAILURE_SURFACE_CASES)):
- **FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeout → visible error** (regression #90908 multi-model path)
- **FallbackSummaryError ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT) → SILENT** (ClawSweeper P2 regression)
- **FallbackSummaryError mixed reasons → SILENT** (rate_limit + timeout keeps silence)
- **upstream abort signal fired → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)
- **user /stop abort → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; achieve parity for Claude

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK seams.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge` — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** — shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ×5 / initialized ×5 / process exited ×5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** — one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch → after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec → 1.7ms`, `Write → 1.9ms`, `Agent → 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** — the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects — one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: fix(ui): collapse non-terminal internal tool errors
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89683. In Control UI / WebChat, a failed **internal** tool call was promoted to a prominent red **"Tool error"** banner in the chat timeline even when the turn produced a clean final assistant reply, for example a hidden shell search that exits 1 for *no matches* (`toolResult { isError:true, status:"failed", exitCode:1 }`). The banner competed with the real answer and leaked internal tool activity as a product failure.

Codex marks **any** non-zero exec exit as failed and cannot distinguish a benign no-match from a fatal error (verified in the `@openai/codex` runtime: `codex-rs/core/src/tools/events.rs:341-345` returns `Err` for `exit_code != 0`, and `:474-478` sets `ExecCommandStatus::Failed`). So the fix is OpenClaw/UI-side and runtime-agnostic: it normalizes the **chat projection boundary** so a failed internal tool that is not the turn's outcome is not surfaced as primary error content, while keeping full detail in the existing expand/activity view.

## What it does

- `buildChatItems` now stamps each tool `MessageGroup` with a `turnSucceeded` signal (a single backward pass: a failed tool group whose turn still produced an assistant **reply with text**, before the next user message, is non-terminal).
- `grouped-render` gates the error promotion (`hasError` / `toolMessageHasError`, which drive the red `--error` styling, the "Tool error" label, the error badge, and auto-expand) on `turnSucceeded !== true`.
- Non-terminal internal tool failures render as a normal collapsed tool summary; the failed output stays available on expand. **Genuinely terminal tool failures** (no assistant reply follows, such as aborted/in-progress turns) still surface as before, and `showToolCalls: false` still hides tool activity entirely.

## Collision check

- No direct competing PR currently claims or closes #89683; this PR is the only direct fix found for the reported Control UI / WebChat rendering issue.
- #89975 is related but complementary: it covers the outbound auto-reply / direct-chat dispatch surface, while this PR covers the Control UI / WebChat render path. There is no file overlap.

## Boundary choice

The Codex runtime correctly reports non-zero internal tool exits as failed. The UI renderer has the additional turn-completion context needed to decide whether that failed internal tool should be the primary visible outcome. This keeps terminal failures prominent while collapsing non-terminal internal failures after a clean assistant reply.

## Verification

Local, Node 22, real `main` base:
- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` + `build-chat-items.test.ts` - **98 passed** (9 new cases)
- `tsgo` (`test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json`), `oxfmt`, `oxlint`, `git diff --check` - clean

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** failed internal tool calls must not render as a primary red error banner once the turn produced a clean assistant reply (#89683); terminal tool failures must still surface.
- **Real environment tested:** rendered the production chat render path (`renderMessageGroup`) into a real DOM (jsdom) on Node v22.14.0 off the current `main`, the same lit render path the Control UI dashboard uses. The transcript mirrors the report: a `shell` `toolResult` with `{ status:"failed", exitCode:1 }` (`isError:true`) whose turn produced a reply, vs. the same failure with no reply.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** rendered the failed tool group through `renderMessageGroup` twice, once as `buildChatItems` now annotates it for a turn that replied (`turnSucceeded:true`) and once as the pre-fix projection emitted it (`turnSucceeded` unset), then read the `.chat-tool-msg-summary` node.
- **Evidence after fix (copied runtime DOM output from the production view):**
```json
{
  "before_turnSucceeded_undefined": {
    "summaryClass": "chat-tool-msg-summary chat-tool-msg-summary--error",
    "label": "Tool error",
    "errorBadge": true
  },
  "after_turnSucceeded_true": {
    "summaryClass": "chat-tool-msg-summary",
    "label": "Tool output",
    "errorBadge": false
  }
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** when the turn replied, the failed internal tool renders as a neutral collapsed "Tool output" summary (no `--error` class, no error badge), so the assistant's answer stays the prominent content; the failed status/output is still shown on expand. When no reply follows, the red "Tool error" banner is unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** a live Control UI browser screenshot against a running gateway (no gateway/dashboard session on this host); reproduced via the production `renderMessageGroup` view in a real DOM instead.

target.title: fix(ui): key skills lists with repeat() so toggle state survives filtering (#89661)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89661. In the Skills panel, flipping a skill's toggle leaves the toggle in
that state for the skill that takes its place. Example: in the **Disabled** tab,
enabling the first skill removes it from the tab, but the next skill now shows an
enabled-looking toggle.

**Root cause:** `ui/src/ui/views/skills.ts` renders the skill groups and the
per-group skill list with lit's `.map()` and no keys. lit reuses DOM nodes by
position, so when a toggled skill leaves the current tab, its `<input class="skill-toggle">`
node — carrying the `checked` state the browser set on click — is reused for the
skill that shifts into that slot. Because the bound `?.checked` value for that
position is unchanged, lit skips the update and the stale checkbox state shows on
the wrong skill.

**Fix:** render both lists with lit's keyed `repeat()` directive
(`group.id` for groups, `skill.skillKey` for skills) so lit tracks nodes by skill
identity instead of position. A filtered-out skill's node is removed rather than
reused, so no toggle state leaks onto its neighbor.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a skill toggle's checked state leaking onto the skill that replaces it when the skill leaves the current tab.

Real environment tested: rendered the production `renderSkills()` Control UI view into a real DOM (jsdom) on macOS, Node 22.22.3, off `origin/main` — the real lit render path the dashboard uses. The reproduction mirrors the reported steps: render the Disabled tab with two disabled skills, flip the first skill's checkbox on (the browser mutates the `<input>`), then re-render with that skill now enabled so it leaves the Disabled tab, and read the remaining toggle.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: render the Disabled tab with `[Skill A, Skill B]` (both disabled), set `checkbox[0].checked = true` to mimic the user click, then re-render with Skill A enabled (filtered out of the tab) and inspect the remaining `input.skill-toggle` state. Ran the same render against pre-fix (`.map`) and post-fix (`repeat`) code.

Evidence after fix (copied runtime output from the production view rendered in a real DOM):

```text
# pre-fix (.map, positional DOM reuse)
[before] Disabled tab shows [Skill A, Skill B]; toggle checked states = [false,false]
[action] user flips Skill A ON (checkbox[0].checked = true); Skill A is now enabled and leaves the Disabled tab
[after]  Disabled tab now shows [Skill B] only; toggle checked states = [true]
[result] FAIL — Skill B's toggle is true (stale state leaked from Skill A's reused node)

# post-fix (keyed repeat)
[before] Disabled tab shows [Skill A, Skill B]; toggle checked states = [false,false]
[action] user flips Skill A ON (checkbox[0].checked = true); Skill A is now enabled and leaves the Disabled tab
[after]  Disabled tab now shows [Skill B] only; toggle checked states = [false]
[result] PASS — Skill B's toggle is OFF (correct); no leaked state from Skill A
```

Observed result after fix: the skill that shifts into the vacated slot keeps its own toggle state. Pre-fix, the reused `<input>` node carried Skill A's "on" state onto Skill B; post-fix, Skill B's toggle stays off.

What was not tested: a live Control UI browser screenshot (no running gateway/dashboard session on this host). Reproduced via the production `renderSkills` view rendered in a real DOM instead.

## Supplemental checks (not a substitute for the proof above)

- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts` — 5/5 pass, including a new regression test that renders the real view and fails without this patch (`expected true to be false`).
- Sibling suites (`ui/src/ui/controllers/skills.test.ts`, `agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts`) — 27/27 pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` exit 0; `oxfmt --check` + oxlint clean.

## Files
- `ui/src/ui/views/skills.ts` — key the groups + skills lists with `repeat()`
- `ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts` — regression test for the toggle-state leak

target.title: fix(anthropic): resolve Claude Haiku 4.5 static catalog refs
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90088.

- Adds the missing native Anthropic API-key static catalog entry for Claude Haiku 4.5.
- Maps rolling/dotted Haiku 4.5 refs to Anthropic's dated API id, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`.
- Keeps this scoped to the `anthropic` API-key provider; `claude-cli` parity is intentionally out of scope.
- Preserves manifest `maxTokens` and `mediaInput` metadata through the agent catalog boundary, closing a pre-existing metadata propagation gap exposed by this model row.

Reviewers should focus on static catalog resolution, Anthropic alias normalization, pricing-cache lookup for the dated Haiku id, and cron/session model override canonicalization.

## Linked context

Closes #90088.

Related PRs checked:
- #87719 changes Haiku fallback/migration behavior, but does not add the missing native Anthropic catalog row.
- #88587 improves prefixed Anthropic normalization context, but does not add `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` to the native Anthropic static catalog.

Direct collision check:
- #90106 adds dated catalog rows, but does not resolve the rolling `claude-haiku-4-5` alias and has no focused regression proof.
- #90116 adds rolling and dated manifest rows, but does not cover dotted aliases, canonical pricing, runtime catalog tests, or proof.
- This PR keeps one canonical dated Anthropic row, uses the existing Opus/Sonnet alias-to-canonical-id pattern, and verifies dated, rolling, dotted, provider-qualified, pricing, cron/session, and catalog metadata paths.

## Canonicalization choice

Rolling and dotted user refs (`claude-haiku-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4.5`) canonicalize to Anthropic's dated API id, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, matching the existing Anthropic Opus/Sonnet alias normalization pattern. This avoids duplicate catalog rows for one vendor model and keeps runtime/pricing ids unambiguous.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`, `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`, and the dated API id now resolve to the bundled native Anthropic static catalog row instead of falling through as an unknown model.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `codex/issue-90088-anthropic-haiku-catalog`, after `pnpm build` completed `runtime-postbuild: bundled plugin metadata`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source-runtime resolution probe with `node --import tsx` against `resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`, plus the focused regression suites listed below.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Console output from the after-patch source-runtime probe:

```text
claude-haiku-4-5 -> anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 input=text,image context=200000 max=64000
claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 -> anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 input=text,image context=200000 max=64000
anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 -> anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 input=text,image context=200000 max=64000
```

- Observed result after fix: Each tested Haiku 4.5 ref resolved to `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` with text/image input, 200k context, and 64k max output from the bundled Anthropic manifest metadata.
- What was not tested: Live Anthropic completion was not tested because no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` was present locally. `claude-cli` catalog parity was not changed or tested because #90088 is scoped to the native Anthropic API-key provider.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The real behavior proof is local source-runtime resolution, not a paid Anthropic API request. Pricing behavior is covered by the gateway pricing-cache regression using OpenRouter-style catalog data.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The issue/review identified that the native Anthropic static catalog lacked the Haiku 4.5 dated row, so rolling Haiku 4.5 refs could not resolve to a bundled native Anthropic row.

## Tests and validation

Focused tests run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.cron-model-override.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.test.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.cron-model-override.test.ts`

Supplemental gates run:

- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cron/isolated-agent/run.cron-model-override.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json packages/model-catalog-core/src/provider-model-id-normalization.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.ts src/agents/model-ref-shared.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.test.ts src/gateway/model-pricing-cache.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed --staged`

Regression coverage added/updated:

- Static catalog resolution for dated, rolling, dotted, and provider-qualified Haiku 4.5 refs.
- Agent model-ref fallback expectations now use the dated canonical id.
- Cron isolated-agent session override expectation now uses the dated canonical id.
- Manifest catalog loading asserts Haiku metadata, including image input, reasoning, 200k context, and 64k max output.
- Gateway pricing cache now proves dated Haiku 4.5 resolves to non-zero pricing via the `version-dots` transform.

Known unrelated local gate failures:

- `pnpm format:check` fails repo-wide on pre-existing unrelated files.
- `pnpm lint:all` fails repo-wide on unrelated generated/app/test files, mostly `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/.../CanvasA2UI/a2ui.bundle.js`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Native Anthropic Haiku 4.5 refs now resolve from the bundled static catalog.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model id canonicalization and pricing lookup for Anthropic Haiku 4.5.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change follows the existing Anthropic opus/sonnet alias pattern, adds focused normalization/static-catalog/model-resolution tests, and adds pricing-cache coverage for the dated Haiku id.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer/reviewer review.

Live Anthropic API proof is not included because no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` was available locally; the included proof exercises the source-runtime static catalog path that caused #90088.

Addressed review items:

- Updated stale `model-ref-shared`, embedded runner model, and cron isolated-agent expectations.
- Expanded static catalog alias coverage.
- Proved non-zero pricing-cache lookup for dated Haiku 4.5.
- Added comments justifying all-provider `maxTokens`/`mediaInput` propagation and core alias duplication.
- Ran the changed-lane gate locally in child mode after default delegated runner sanity checks failed locally.

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; achieve parity for Claude

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK seams.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge` — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** — shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ×5 / initialized ×5 / process exited ×5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** — one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch → after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec → 1.7ms`, `Write → 1.9ms`, `Agent → 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** — the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects — one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: fix(memory): honor local model path in index identity
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes local memory-search indexes being reported dirty after a rebuild when `agents.*.memorySearch.provider` resolves to the local embedding adapter and uses a configured `local.modelPath`.
- Covers both direct `provider = "local"` and configured provider aliases whose adapter id resolves to `local`.

Why does this matter now?

- #91001 reports that `openclaw memory status` still shows `Dirty: yes` after `openclaw memory index --force` with local embeddings, leaving vector search paused even though the index was rebuilt.

What is the intended outcome?

- Status-only memory managers compare index identity against the same effective local model path that the local embedding provider writes into index metadata.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No local embedding runtime, node-llama-cpp, vector-store, or CLI formatting behavior changes.

What does success look like?

- A rebuilt local index using `local.modelPath` reports a valid index identity and `dirty: false` when reopened through the status path before provider initialization.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The status-only identity comparison in `manager-sync-ops.ts` and the direct plus alias regression coverage in `index.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91001

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90413

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request found; issue is labeled `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, and `clawsweeper:source-repro`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: status-only memory index identity checks used `memorySearch.model`/adapter defaults for local embeddings even though the local provider records `local.modelPath` as the indexed model.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-91001`, Node `v24.16.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, isolated temporary OpenClaw home/state/config, `node-llama-cpp`, and a real local `nomic-embed-text-v1.5-Q4_0.gguf` model file.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node ./openclaw.mjs memory index --force --agent main`
  - `node ./openclaw.mjs memory status --agent main --json`
  - `node ./openclaw.mjs memory status --agent main --deep --json`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live terminal output from the isolated local GGUF run:

  ```text
  $ node ./openclaw.mjs memory index --force --agent main
  Memory index updated (main).

  $ node ./openclaw.mjs memory status --agent main --json
  provider=local
  requestedProvider=local
  model=None
  dirty=False
  files=1
  chunks=1
  indexIdentity={'status': 'valid'}
  dbPath=/tmp/openclaw-91010-live-proof/state/memory-main.sqlite
  workspaceDir=/tmp/openclaw-91010-live-proof/workspace

  $ node ./openclaw.mjs memory status --agent main --deep --json
  provider=local
  requestedProvider=local
  model=<redacted-tmp-model>/nomic-embed-text-v1.5-Q4_0.gguf
  dirty=False
  files=1
  chunks=1
  indexIdentity={'status': 'valid'}
  embeddingProbe={'ok': True}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the real CLI local embedding rebuild/status flow leaves the status path clean, and the focused regression now covers direct `local` plus a provider alias that resolves to the local adapter.
- What was not tested: vector-store semantic search with sqlite-vec enabled; the live proof used the built-in SQLite index with vector store disabled to isolate the provider/model identity behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live GGUF proof used a temporary isolated OpenClaw home/state/config and a proof-only local source build/runtime install. Paths above are temporary proof paths and the model path is redacted in the summary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #91001 shows the before behavior: after local rebuild, `Dirty: yes` and index identity reported a built/expected mismatch or missing metadata.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node ./openclaw.mjs memory index --force --agent main`
- `node ./openclaw.mjs memory status --agent main --json`
- `node ./openclaw.mjs memory status --agent main --deep --json`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-final3-91010-$(date +%s) timeout 240s pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t '#91001' --reporter=verbose --passWithNoTests=false --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Updated `keeps status clean when local modelPath is the indexed model (#91001)` to cover both direct `provider: "local"` and a configured provider alias resolving to the local adapter.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A status-only manager expected the adapter/configured model while metadata stored the local provider's effective `local.modelPath`, so status could report the rebuilt index dirty.
- The first version of this PR also missed aliases whose resolved adapter id is `local`; that is now covered.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Memory index identity status for local embeddings and local-provider aliases.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change is scoped to the status/identity expectation for providers whose resolved adapter id is `local` and `local.modelPath` is set; existing provider-initialized behavior is unchanged, focused direct/alias regressions pass, and a real local GGUF CLI rebuild/status flow now proves `dirty: false`.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- ClawSweeper re-review and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed ClawSweeper feedback requesting live local GGUF proof and alias-safe handling for `models.providers.<id>.api` resolving to `local`.

target.title: fix: cap sessions history and compact session status
target.body:
Spec #241 upstream source integration.

- Adds config-backed limits for sessions_history includeTools replay
- Adds compact/full session_status details mode
- Updates schema/help/labels/docs/tests
- Rebases onto current upstream main and fixes two unrelated CI hygiene failures in extension code

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Iggy was reachable but session context grew too quickly because `sessions_history includeTools:true` could replay large tool results and `session_status` duplicated full status text in `details.statusText`. This patch caps tool-inclusive history, summarizes tool results, and makes `session_status` compact by default with an explicit full-details opt-in.

**Real environment tested:** Juan's local OpenClaw setup on macOS, 2026-06-06 ET. Installed runtime stayed on OpenClaw `2026.6.1`; this PR was validated in the upstream source clone before installing it into the local runtime, to avoid another local-only runtime patch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran live OpenClaw/Iggy reachability probes against the real local setup, then validated the source branch with the focused sessions/status tests, prod/test typechecks, lint, extension boundary checks, formatting, and whitespace checks.

**Evidence after fix:** Live output from the real local setup:

```console
$ openclaw gateway status --json | jq '{loaded:.service.loaded,runtime:.service.runtime.status,rpc:.rpc.ok,version:.gateway.version,pluginDrifts:(.pluginVersionDrift.drifts|length)}'
{
  "loaded": true,
  "runtime": "running",
  "rpc": true,
  "version": "2026.6.1",
  "pluginDrifts": 0
}

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3333/api/debug | jq '{gatewayConnected,pendingRequests,activeTurns,version:.gateway.version}'
{
  "gatewayConnected": true,
  "pendingRequests": 0,
  "activeTurns": null,
  "version": null
}

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3333/api/sessions | jq '{count:length, busy:([.[]? | select(.busy == true)] | length), withPending:([.[]? | select(.pendingChatRunId != null)] | length)}'
{
  "count": 17,
  "busy": 0,
  "withPending": 0
}

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/ | head -c 80
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
```

Source branch validation output:

```console
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/config/config.tools-alsoAllow.test.ts
4 test files, 104 tests passed across 2 shards

$ pnpm tsgo:prod
pass

$ pnpm tsgo:test
pass

$ pnpm lint:core
pass

$ pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled
pass

$ pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
extension package boundary check passed; compiled plugins: 114

$ pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary
extension package boundary check passed; canary plugins: 2

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/concepts/session-tool.md src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/config/types.tools.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/config.tools-alsoAllow.test.ts extensions/acpx/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/device-pair/notify.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ git diff --check
pass
```

**Observed result after fix:** The real local gateway/Iggy setup was reachable and drained (`gatewayConnected=true`, `pendingRequests=0`, `busy=0`, `withPending=0`) while the source branch now enforces the durable context-bloat controls in upstream code instead of relying on a local runtime patch.

**What was not tested:** The PR branch was not installed into Juan's live OpenClaw runtime. That was intentionally avoided until upstream review/merge so the local setup can return to vanilla and avoid drift.

Known CI note: local `pnpm check:architecture` still reports an existing madge cycle through agents/channels/config on current upstream main. That failure is outside the Spec #241 context-limit patch and was not introduced by these changed files.

target.title: perf(tui): prewarm runtime plugins before first send
target.body:
## Profile Evidence

| Run | CPU profile | Startup ready | Submit -> first model request | `startup.runtime-plugins` on first submit | First-turn embedded duration | Request -> visible response |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Before | `.artifacts/tui-local-cpu/full-surface-1780700761191/openclaw-tui-99042-2026-06-05T23-06-02-328Z.cpuprofile` | 4,494ms | 12,683ms | 5,164ms | 10,824ms | 17ms |
| After | `.artifacts/tui-local-cpu/full-surface-runtime-prewarm-1780701756700/openclaw-tui-98266-2026-06-05T23-22-43-120Z.cpuprofile` | 10,929ms | 7,412ms | 1ms | 3,859ms | 20ms |

The local mocked Responses server still returned in ~20ms, so the user-visible freeze is before provider I/O. This patch moves the broad `ensureRuntimePluginsLoaded` cold activation out of the first submit path and into initial TUI history load, blocking submit with `warming runtime` until the warmup settles.

## Summary

- Add an optional embedded TUI backend `prewarmAgentRuntime` hook that calls `ensureRuntimePluginsLoaded` for the current session/agent workspace.
- Start that prewarm during the first history load and show `warming runtime` while it runs.
- Wire the real submit gate to block sends during `warming runtime`; this avoids the caller-path gap found on #86981.
- Keep failures best-effort: history still loads, and a prewarm failure is rendered as a system message.

## Relationship to #86981

#86981 prewarms the selected plugin harness runtime and is stacked on the closed #86930 base hook. In this repro the selected harness is the built-in OpenClaw runtime, so that approach does not remove the measured `ensureRuntimePluginsLoaded` spike. This PR targets the measured local embedded TUI stage directly and stands alone on current `main`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
- Profiled `pnpm openclaw tui --local` with an isolated config and mocked local Responses endpoint before and after the patch.

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): resolve path/socket from the state dir (honor OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `exec-approvals.{json,sock}` were resolved from the home default instead of the canonical OpenClaw state dir.
- Relocated installs using `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` could split approval state or fail exec calls when `$HOME/.openclaw` was symlinked.
- Changed files: `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts`, `src/config/paths.ts`, `src/infra/state-dir.ts`.

Why does this matter now?

- `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is documented and supported, but exec approvals were not honoring that state-dir override.

What is the intended outcome?

- Exec approvals resolve through `resolveStateDir()`.
- Default installs keep the same `<home>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.*` paths.
- Relocated installs use `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` for approval storage.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Approval policy, command analysis, socket protocol behavior, and config-supplied `socket.path` overrides.

What does success look like?

- Default installs keep existing behavior.
- Relocated installs keep approval files and sockets under the configured state dir.
- Symlinked state-dir components are still refused by the exec-approval symlink guard.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The state-dir ownership boundary in `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`.
- The trusted-root selection for the symlink guard.
- The default-path and relocated-path regression coverage.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90971

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- Related state-dir consumers: `src/infra/device-auth-store.ts`, `src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts`, `src/infra/push-web.ts`.
- Related docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/help/environment/

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Yes, owner request.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: exec-approvals path resolution under `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, including symlinked-home relocated installs.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node 24.16.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.ts src/infra/state-dir.ts`; `git diff --check --cached`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).`; `[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 11.85s`; `All matched files use the correct format.`
- Observed result after fix: approvals resolve to the configured state dir, default installs keep the same paths, and the symlink guard still refuses a symlinked state-dir component.
- What was not tested: full multi-host gateway/live approval run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local macOS proof only; GitHub CI will re-run on the pushed PR head. The broad Madge import-cycle check still reports an existing upstream-main SCC and is not claimed as passing here.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): relocated installs previously wrote approvals to `$HOME/.openclaw`, and symlinked-home setups could fail exec calls with `Refusing to traverse symlink in exec approvals path`.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts` -> `0 runtime value cycle(s)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.test.ts` -> 3 shards, 3 files, 42 tests passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.ts src/infra/state-dir.ts` -> clean.
- `git diff --check --cached` -> clean.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts` coverage for HOME-only defaults, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` precedence, and relocated symlink refusal.
- Updated `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts` expectations for state-dir-aware approval paths.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Relocated installs used `$HOME/.openclaw` instead of `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- Symlinked-home relocated installs could fail exec calls with the symlink guard refusal.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and updated for the touched behavior.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. Relocated-state installs now read and write exec approvals in `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`; default installs keep the same paths.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` now controls exec-approval storage like the other state-owned files. No new config key or migration was added.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. This touches command-execution approval storage only; approval policy and socket protocol are unchanged, and symlink refusal is preserved.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Trusted-root selection for the approval path symlink guard.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Default installs keep a trusted root equivalent to the previous home root.
- Relocated installs are covered by state-dir precedence and symlink-refusal tests.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- CI and maintainer review on the scoped 5-file PR head.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- GitHub CI on `3f7e17d2825f21184ce7fbf7c774171098661947`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- PR scope and body were repaired for the updated 5-file branch.

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>

target.title: perf(qqbot): narrow tool discovery cold load
target.body:
## Profile Evidence

| Run | CPU profile | Submit -> first model request | Request -> visible response | QQBot tool-discovery/register | First-turn embedded duration |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Before | `.artifacts/tui-local-cpu/full-surface-1780700761191/openclaw-tui-99042-2026-06-05T23-06-02-328Z.cpuprofile` | 12,683ms | 17ms | 4,640.8ms | 10,824ms |
| After | `.artifacts/tui-local-cpu/full-surface-narrow-tools-1780700900305/openclaw-tui-17302-2026-06-05T23-08-29-149Z.cpuprofile` | 10,387ms | 23ms | 1,268.9ms | 6,563ms |

CPU profile summaries showed the first turn is cold module loading, not provider latency: top samples were `dist/jiti.cjs`, Babel transforms, `package_json_reader`, `fs` stat/open/read, and ESM compile. The mocked local Responses server returned in ~20ms; the delay happened before the request was sent.

## Summary

- Route QQBot `tool-discovery` registration through the narrow tool registration module instead of the broad public `api.ts` barrel.
- Keep full QQBot registration on the channel-entry path for normal runtime registration.
- Lazy-load the QQ sender auth helper only when `qqbot_channel_api` executes, so tool registration does not import messaging sender/runtime code.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/commands/framework-registration.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/tools/remind.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/tools/remind-logic.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-entry-contract.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
- Profiled `pnpm openclaw tui --local` with an isolated config and mocked local Responses endpoint before and after the patch.

target.title: fix(imessage): self-explaining private-API failures and dedicated send timeout
target.body:
## Summary

Two macOS-26-resilience fixes for the iMessage channel:

1. **Surface *why* the private API is unavailable.** The probe now carries imsg's own `status --json` `message` (SIP / library validation / macOS 26 AMFI gate) into `IMessagePrivateApiStatus.statusMessage`, and blocked actions append it. Operators on macOS 26.5 stop getting the misleading "run imsg launch" with no reason.

2. **Decouple the send timeout from the probe timeout.** Sends inherited the 10s probe default via `client.request`. On macOS 26 the private-API bridge intermittently stalls up to ~124s; the 10s abort dropped attachment/reply sends (non-recoverable shapes). New `DEFAULT_IMESSAGE_SEND_TIMEOUT_MS = 150_000` for sends only (covers the observed upper bound + headroom); probes/health checks stay fast. **No new config surface** — explicit opts/`probeTimeoutMs` still win. Mirrors the BlueBubbles send-timeout fix (#69193).

3. **Companion imsg bridge fix.** ClawSweeper correctly found that OpenClaw's 150s client wait was incomplete while upstream `imsg` bridge sends still defaulted to 10s. Companion PR open: openclaw/imsg#139 changes send-style bridge actions (`send-message`, `send-multipart`, `send-attachment`, `send-poll`) to use a 150s bridge response timeout while keeping non-send RPC waits at 10s and preserving explicit timeout overrides.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
- Full imessage extension suite: **548 tests pass**, incl. 2 new tests (statusMessage propagation; 150s send default).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/status.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/actions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts` (3 files, 81 tests passed).
- Codex `$autoreview` clean (it caught an initial 60s-too-short default vs the documented 124s stall window; fixed to 150s).
- **Dependency source inspected:** local sibling imsg source had `IMsgBridgeProtocol.defaultResponseTimeout = 10.0`, and `RPCServer` / CLI bridge send paths called `IMsgBridgeClient.shared.invoke` without a longer send timeout. Companion openclaw/imsg#139 fixes that dependency-layer timeout contract.
- **Companion imsg verification for openclaw/imsg#139:**
  - `swift format lint Sources/IMsgCore/IMsgBridgeClient.swift Sources/IMsgCore/IMsgBridgeProtocol.swift Tests/IMsgCoreTests/IMsgBridgeProtocolTests.swift`
  - `DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer swift test --filter IMsgBridgeProtocolTests`
  - `DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer swift test --filter RPCBridgeMessageHandlersTests --filter RPCServerBridgeTests --filter BridgeRichCommandTests`
  - `DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer make test` (325 tests in 2 suites passed)
- **Live macOS 26.4.1 status proof on this machine:** `imsg status --json` returned `message: "Connected to Messages.app. IMCore features available."`, `v2_ready: true`, and `bridge_version: 2`; `pnpm openclaw channels status --probe imessage --json` reported iMessage configured/running with `probe.ok: true` and `privateApi.available: true`. This host is healthy, so it cannot produce the blocked-action error text without intentionally breaking the bridge; the unavailable-message path is covered by the focused unit tests above.
- **Live regression smoke on macOS 26.4.1 (lobster):** built 2026.6.1 + this fix, deployed to the gateway, restarted (PID bounced), iMessage + Telegram both healthy, no startup errors.
